column20: Camps are for ages 6 – teen. This is a beginner/intermediate aerial camp perfect for those brand new to aerial or with less than a year of aerial experience. In the week long camp your kiddo will learn technique and skills on 4 apparatuses: silks, trapeze, hoop, sling. All our instructors are certified and have experience teaching youth aerialists - our focus is on safety and fun! There are half day camp options and full day camp options.

column18:
Each day, campers will focus on a particular area of the Farm, learning how to care for animals, plant seeds, and be stewards of the land. Animal interactions including engaging and working with sheep, goats, chickens, rabbits, donkey and miniature horses. Campers will get their hands in the dirty while gardening in soil and then travel to the greenhouse to learn the magic and science behind growing food with Aeroponics and Aquaponics. We offer a range of authentic, hands-on activities that invite participation in the farm’s day-to-day operations. In addition, their time here will include crafts, and games supporting what they learned and experienced day. 

Horse camp (only half day, full day starts at 8)
Very Young Riders (Ages 5-7, No experience necessary): 
July 11- June 15 | July 18 - July 22 (9:00 am - 12:00pm)
Cost: TUF Family Members- $320 | Non-Member Early Bird - $340 | Open Registration - $350

Camp is 100% outdoors, with all the mud, sun, dirt, rain, and bugs campers could want. If the weather is bad we hold camp under shelter. 

Camp groups will be four to eight campers and one counselor. Small groups often pair up to do activities or play games. 

Daily activities vary by session, but typically include one craft and one activity per day, and we always spend some time wading in Sand Creek to cool down and explore.

column20:
Performance Camps are perfect for students who love being on stage but aren't ready for the commitment of a full-length production. In just one week, campers will dive into a dynamic theatre experience, learning 3 to 4 exciting songs complete with music and choreography, as well as several engaging short scenes based on the camp's theme.
Storybook Theatre camp offers a magical experience for young actors as they bring a beloved children's book to life on stage. Throughout the week, campers will learn essential theatre skills like blocking, projection, and teamwork, while also enjoying creative activities such as crafting costumes. With songs, dances, and lively scenes, the camp culminates in a final performance on Friday at 11:30 am. Whether new to acting or experienced, this camp is a fun way to build confidence, improve public speaking, and make lasting friendships!
column21: There are also some specialty camps such as Acting for Screens, Improv Games, Playwriting, and Lego Masters & Bluey Camps!

At Steve & Kate’s, campers choose which activities they do, whom they do it with and for how long every day. 
Every week we supplement our main offering of activities with thrill-inducing weekly specials.
column20:
All materials, hours, lunch and snacks included. 
Coding. Sewing. Baking. Making. Sporting. Filming. Lounging. Every day, campers face the excruciating task of choosing from these and many more engrossing activities. Some select a mix of many. Others burrow deep into one or two. Either way, each day they face new experiences bristling with trade-offs and tantalizing possibilities.

In the early morning and late afternoon, campers ramp up and wind down with recreational choices.

Little Wellness Retreats  - A weekly creative + mindfulness experience for kids!

Kids get to paint on the walls without limits or bounds and create tangible art projects rooted in mindfulness. Each week explores a new theme like heart, breath, roots, goals and more. 

Kids move their bodies through gentle yoga, learn simple emotional-regulation tools and start tuning into their own intuition in fun, accessible ways.

This isn’t just for “calm” or “zen” kids, it’s for every kid who benefits from space to create, connect and feel their feelings in a healthy, supported way.

column20: Hey! I just dropped my son off at a music camp we sort of found by chance and it looks awesome! They rented the Dolce Event Center off 6th and chambers and filled it with so many instruments and DJ equipment.  The two guys running it seems great, but I think they struggled with advertising because there aren't a ton of kids.  They're still open to taking kiddos of any age this week, or individual days for $50 per day (including today if you want to get out of the heat). Drop off is 8-8:45 and pickup is at 5.  It really looks like a lot of fun, thought I'd see if anyone is looking for an activity this week. Comment here if interested and I'll send you the directors contact info. (It's also called Apex music camp if you want to find them online)

column20:
Since 2015 we've been taking kids on field trips over summer break, school holidays, and select weekends and evenings. 
We travel together in one vehicle which means siblings can always stay together! This also offers kids the rare and fun opportunity to make friends with kids of different ages. By demonstrating respect and friendliness and going to awesome destinations, we're able to make sure that kids love coming on our adventures! And with all the running, swimming and playing around, your kids are likely to be exhausted at pick up. We are also insured to provide tour services for adults and are currently doing so per special request.
